selection_policy.promotion_rule: reward-trained checkpoint must beat the BC fallback on mean_reward across independent 1000-seed windows without regressing deal-in rate; win rate is tracked as a secondary metric.
selection_policy.serving_validation: Python returns an action_id from visible SeatObservation inputs; Go must still decode and validate the action_id against current legal actions before applying it.

notes: Promoted for Chongci only; the classic Fenghua reward-trained best remains current_reward_trained_best.
This promotion uses direct tensor-bearing counterfactual pairs from the same combined-gate divergence traces that rejected the EV-up/tail-worse risk-regularized candidates.
Candidate and previous-best repeats matched exactly on the combined gate, so the promotion is not due to evaluation drift.
The candidate improves mean reward by 0.05336, positive rate by 2.50pp, and large-loss rate by -0.83pp versus the previous Chongci anchor on the deterministic combined gate.

current_chongci_risk_critic_candidate.reason: The fb025 future-context risk critic has the best offline large-loss AUC so far and threshold 0.60 slightly beat anchor on the calibration/smoke window, but the independent seed660000 duplicate gate regressed mean reward, positive rate, and large-loss rate. A policy-nearest substitute ranking recovered some mean reward but still lost to pure anchor and did not improve tail risk. Keep the checkpoint for analysis/testing only.

rejected_chongci_risk_critic_candidates.reason: The stronger family-balance/window-12 critic increased probability scale but did not improve independent ranking. Overall AUC and discard AUC were both slightly worse than the prior fb025 critic on the same capped400k calibration protocol, and Brier was much worse, so constrained policy selection was not run.

The family-specific later-outcome target is the best offline action-risk calibration so far, but both strict and discard-tolerant tail-constrained selection lost EV to the anchor on the seed660000:4 smoke while preserving the same positive and large-loss rates. Keep the target implementation and use the critic for diagnostics; do not promote or run the larger gate with this candidate pair.

rejected_candidates.reason: The targeted combined-gate divergence branch avoided broad scalar loss stacking and produced an EV/positive-rate gain after replay-expanding exact divergence rows, but it still regressed large-loss rate from the anchor 15.00% to 16.67%. Per the explicit tail constraint, this checkpoint is not promotable.

Adding large_loss_bc_weight=0.50 to the previously promising t054/w075 discard risk regularizer worsened the combined gate: mean reward regressed by 0.0261 and large-loss rate regressed by 5.0pp versus anchor. The large-loss BC term was active during training, but it did not preserve tail outcomes in duplicate evaluation.

The stronger t054/w075 variant passed the 660000:16 gate with EV and positive-rate gains and no large-loss regression, but failed the combined 534000/544000/554000 gate: mean reward worsened by 0.0114 and large-loss rate worsened by 2.5pp. The lower-threshold t0535/w050 variant was already rejected at 660000:16 for large-loss regression.

Direct serving improved EV and positive rate on the seed660000:16 gate but increased large-loss rate from 17.19% to 18.75%. The explicit tail-constrained selector restored anchor large-loss rate but lost EV versus the anchor, so neither direct nor constrained serving should be promoted.

Rejected because direct counterfactual pairwise IQL improved mean reward and positive-reward rate on the independent 660000:16 duplicate gate but regressed large-loss rate from the promoted anchor 17.19% to 20.31%.
notes: The direct pairwise-data path is mechanically active and useful tooling because exact replay matching found zero rows for the older datasets.
Do not scale this exact 269-row auxiliary ratio without a new tail-control ingredient; it repeats the EV-up/tail-risk-up failure mode seen in the external-risk regularizer branch.

rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because restricting direct counterfactual pairwise IQL to high-risk rows still improved mean reward and positive-reward rate while regressing large-loss rate from the promoted anchor 17.19% to 20.31%.
notes: High-risk filtering did not fix the EV-up/tail-risk-up failure mode.
The next branch needs a true tail constraint or severity-aware target, not only narrower pairwise margin rows.

rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because reward-delta severity pairwise IQL improved mean reward and reduced tail regression versus equal-margin pairwise runs, but still regressed large-loss rate from the promoted anchor 17.19% to 18.75%.
notes: This is the first direct pairwise variant that moved large-loss rate down from 20.31% to 18.75%.
Try one stronger severity-margin variant before abandoning reward-delta pairwise constraints.

rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because stronger reward-delta severity margins regressed large-loss rate back to 20.31% and lost the positive-rate gain, despite better mean reward.
notes: Moderate severity margin 0.35 remains the best pairwise-tail variant but still fails the anchor tail guard.
Stop pairwise-margin sweeps; next work should use explicit constrained selection or large-loss probability constraints.

rejected_candidates.reason: Training-side discard risk regularization improved mean reward and positive rate on seed660000 but regressed large-loss rate by 3.125 percentage points. Tail-only behavior-cloning preservation with weights 1.0 and 3.0 reduced EV gain but still did not recover large-loss rate; do not promote or widen this exact candidate family.
Balanced positive/negative risk training improved probability scale and severity error but large-loss AUC remained random, so no guarded online evaluation was run.
The 58-scalar visible match-history context improved Brier slightly but large-loss AUC remained near random, so no guarded online evaluation was run.
The first action-conditioned risk critic was mechanically active, but offline large-loss calibration remained near random, so no guarded online evaluation was run.
Lower auxiliary coefficients reproduced the previous all-anchor auxiliary result and still regressed large-loss rate versus the promoted anchor.
Large-loss auxiliary training was active and improved EV versus replay-only all-anchor runs, but it regressed large-loss rate versus the promoted anchor.
Sparse-row oversampling exposed matched rows during training but did not improve mean reward, positive rate, or large-loss rate versus the all-anchor filtered run.
Expanding filtered replay to all 30 dense-trace seeds only kept 16 risk-context rows and worsened mean reward versus the promoted anchor.
Filtered first-divergence replay matched the scalar-only current64 candidate and still trailed the promoted anchor on mean reward; only 11 risk-context rows survived filtering.

rejected_candidates.rejection_reason: Rejected because the wider duplicate-seat gate lost to the current Chongci promoted checkpoint on mean reward, positive-reward rate, and large-loss rate.
notes: The quick seed 413000 screen looked promising on mean reward and large-loss rate, but the wider three-window gate reversed that signal.
Do not use this checkpoint for serving or as the next self-play policy source.

rejected_candidates.rejection_reason: Rejected because the wider duplicate-seat gate lost to the current Chongci promoted checkpoint on mean reward, positive-reward rate, and large-loss rate despite a promising quick screen.
notes: The candidate is nearly identical to the promoted policy on offline rows generated by the promoted policy, but the small differences did not improve online duplicate evaluation.
The next useful work is higher-signal diagnostics or targeted feature/reward changes, not another blind low-drift IQL continuation.

diagnostics.guarded_q_rejection: Q-margin override remained unsafe; q_margin=0.5 still lost to the effective anchor baseline on the failed seed bands.

rejected_candidates.rejection_reason: Rejected because the candidate passed the failed seed-band gate but lost to the current Chongci promoted checkpoint on mean reward, positive-reward rate, and large-loss rate on an independent duplicate-seat gate.
notes: The failed seed-band gate showed useful direction from stronger CQL, BC anchoring, and downside shaping.
Do not promote this checkpoint; use it only as evidence that downside-shaped direct policy training is worth further study with stronger independent validation.

source_generation.policy_source: all seats controlled by current Chongci promoted checkpoint

rejected_candidates.rejection_reason: Rejected because the candidate passed the quick screen but lost to the current Chongci promoted checkpoint on mean reward in the independent duplicate-seat gate.
notes: The candidate improved positive-reward rate and large-loss rate on the independent gate, but expected value remains the primary promotion metric.
The useful signal is that capped current-policy self-play can reduce tail losses; the next attempt should preserve the downside improvement while recovering mean reward.

constraint.rule: allow candidate action only when candidate Q improves and predicted large-loss probability is no worse than anchor action

notes: This serving configuration preserves anchor large-loss rate on both the 660000:16 gate and the combined 534000/544000/554000 gate.
It is rejected as a promotion because the combined gate loses mean reward and positive-reward rate versus the anchor while allowing candidate overrides on only about 0.06% of decisions.
The explicit tail constraint works mechanically, but the current risk model/candidate pair is too conservative to produce useful EV improvement.
